content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN integers.

From array AAA, we create a new array BBB containing all pairwise bitwise ANDs of elements from AAA. That is, BBB consists of N⋅(N−1)/2N\cdot(N-1)/2N⋅(N−1)/2 elements, each of the form Ai&AjA_i \mathbin{\&} A_jAi​&Aj​ for some 1≤i<j≤N1 \leq i \lt j \leq N1≤i<j≤N.

In array BBB, we repeat the following process till there is only one element remaining:


Let the current maximum and minimum element of array BBB be XXX and YYY respectively.
Remove XXX and YYY from array BBB and insert X∣YX \mathbin{|} YX∣Y into it.
Determine the last remaining element in array BBB.

Here, &\mathbin{\&}& denotes the Bitwise AND operation and ∣\mathbin{|}∣ denotes the Bitwise OR operation.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, denoting the number of elements in AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the last remaining element in array BBB.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 2·10^5
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N (2 ≤ N ≤ 10^5), the number of elements in array A. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN (0 ≤ Ai ≤ 10^9).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer – the value that remains after repeatedly applying the described operation on array B.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case we count bits that appear in at least two numbers of A. Test1: only bit0 appears twice → answer 1. Test2: bits0 and1 appear twice → answer 3. Test3: bits1 and2 appear twice → answer 6.
No bit is set in any number, therefore no bit can appear in an AND of a pair. The final OR is 0.
Bits set are: 5→0,2; 2→1; 8→3. Each bit occurs in at most one number, so no bit reaches count≥2. Result is 0.

solution.approach: For each bit position we only need to know whether it appears in at least two numbers of the original array A. A bit is set in Ai & Aj exactly when both Ai and Aj have that bit. The whole process of repeatedly taking the current maximum and minimum and OR‑ing them does not change the overall OR of the multiset, therefore the final element equals the OR of all pairwise ANDs. Consequently a bit will be present in the answer iff it occurs in at least two numbers of A. We count, for each of the 31 possible bit positions (0…30), how many Ai have the bit set and set the bit in the answer when the count is ≥ 2.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N·31) per test case, i.e. O(N) overall (the factor 31 is constant).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(31) auxiliary space for the bit counters.
